methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Sicona Battery Technologies is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Christiaan Jordaan and Andrew Minett, having raised US$22M+ to date. The valuation universe comprises nine peers in the Climate & Energy sector, including one public comparable (LNZA) and eight private companies such as Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen.

A peer-median approach was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables. Filtering the nine sector peers by stage and valuation tier yielded fewer than three datapoints in any narrow cohort, making a Series A–specific median calculation unreliable. An all-sector median would obscure material differences in capital intensity, technology risk, and market timing across energy subsegments and therefore was not pursued.

The analysis cannot support a triangulated valuation at this time. The single public comparable and the broader private peer set provide directional context but do not constitute a sufficient quantitative foundation for a point estimate or tight range. Key uncertainties include the maturity of Sicona's silicon anode technology relative to peers' development stages, the timing and capital requirements for scale-up, and market adoption risk in the battery ecosystem. Any forward valuation will require either (a) expansion of the comparable set to adjacent subsectors, or (b) recourse to income or asset-based methods, both of which lie outside the scope of peer analysis.
